summary: 20-agent contract rebuild design: 8 code-mappers, 4 competing architectures + 7 per-feature specs, adversarial verify, synthesize plan

plan.summary: Hybrid plan; 7 features as additive default-init data reusing the monotonic evaluate (Player.h:621), B8 bidding (GM 1979-2105), and the fa_demand_baseline pattern (clear 285/2324) keyed by const Player. Promise default role is Role::Count not Role::Flex.
plan.chosen_architecture: PERSONALITY-FIRST + MINIMAL, plus cached deterministic baseline to kill propose/evaluate drift. Four Player scalars (ambition/loyalty/greed/ego) + value/perf/accolade helpers, keeping Desire gates. Reject market-driven standing-bid Send; keep instant-sign commits; rivals are read-only display + deadline rival-sign firing only if user dawdles. New fields default-init after canonical Contract fields; signing writes only at the 3 canonical exp_year sites.
data_model_changes: ResignBreakdown: bonus_mod, promise_mod, ask_k, counter fields.

steps.title: Value rebuild
steps.detail: perf_score/accolade_score/value_index replace star/last_season/trophy mults; signature unchanged.
steps.risk: band drift; tune clamps not caps

steps.title: Add scalars
steps.detail: ambition/loyalty/greed/ego=50, seed from attrs+age+desire; neutral no-op.

steps.title: Ask weighting
steps.detail: greed/ego raise ask in team_ctx block; loyalty discounts same_team; floor_frac twin-edit.

steps.title: Decision weighting
steps.detail: loyalty_mod, ambition weak-team penalty, ego below-ask penalty (monotonic-safe), role_mod scale.

steps.title: Bonus+Promise
steps.detail: bonus_mod=salary_mod(eff)-salary_mod(amt); promise_mod cap+20; stamp at canonical sites.

steps.title: Benched promise
steps.detail: starter+active+season_matches==0 -> bump_mood +0.35, clear active; re-arm on played season.

steps.title: Counter card
steps.detail: total_at lambda bisect amount, scan years; fill counter fields; Accept Counter snaps sliders.
steps.test: near-miss round-trip accepts; insult no counter

steps.title: Reject cost
steps.detail: classify tier, bump_mood, decrement offers_left; disable Send at 0; only on Send.

steps.title: Suitors display
steps.detail: fa_suitors_ map; mark_fa_courted seeds B8 candidate scan; show Other interest N teams.

steps.title: Live bidding
steps.detail: per-day +8% escalation to deadline; rival signs via sign_player(gm.year), manual budget debit, set contract.
steps.test: deadline flips to rival or pushes; budget exact

steps.title: Multi-year UI
steps.detail: Signing bonus slider + Guarantee starter checkbox into 6-arg evaluate; commit cost amt+bonus.
steps.test: bonus line rises; longer years shrinks it

steps.title: Regression gate
steps.detail: Run full suite; grep ALL TESTS PASSED (build.bat exit unreliable); re-validate bands; never-free.
steps.test: ALL TESTS PASSED; manual both flows

architectures.angle: MINIMAL/INCREMENTAL: deliver all 7 features as purely additive, default-initialized fields plus one counter-offer band, one suitor loop, and a thin re-weight of the existing gen_contract multiplier chain — touching the three canonical exp_year write sites and DrawNegotiationModal only, with zero changes to any existing call site's signature or aggregate-init usage.
architecture: STATE SPINE (one new struct, one new map): All negotiation state lives in two additive places. (1) A `Contract::Promise` nested aggregate + new defaulted Contract fields carry the *signed* deal terms (multi-year structure, signing bonus, role promise). (2) A new `GameManager` member `std::unordered_map<const Player*, SuitorState> fa_suitors_` (mirrors fa_demand_baseline_k_ exactly: declared/cleared at GameManager.cpp:285,2324) carries *live* offseason contention. No existing struct field is moved or retyped; everything is a new defaulted member.

FEATURE 1 COUNTER-OFFERS (deterministic bisection, no new engine logic): evaluate_resign_offer is already monotonic in amount_k (Player.h:621). Add ONE field `int counter_offer_k = 0;` to ResignBreakdown (Player.h:628, defaulted -> all 3 UI consumers + accepts_resign_offer keep compiling). In the hard_block branch (Player.cpp:3021-3028) and at the will_accept line (3030), when !will_accept compute the smallest amount in [ro.min_acceptable_k .. ro.amount_k*overpay] that flips total>=55 by bisection on the existing pure scorer (deterministic, no rng — satisfies the every-frame constraint). The 'counter' is the player surfacing their existing acceptable envelope, NOT a new number. UI renders it in the reject branch of DrawNegotiationModal (insert after gui_main.cpp:4977, before Columns(1) at 4979).

FEATURE 2 REJECT COST: Add `int negotiation_rounds = 0;` + `int reject_floor_bump_k = 0;` to the per-FA SuitorState (offseason) AND mirror a session-only counter in AppState for the modal. Cost is applied at the Send-Offer reject sites (Resign 5017-5023, FA 5047-5049): each rejected offer bumps a small min_acceptable_k delta on the cached_offer (caller-owned, not engine state) so the next ask is stiffer. Engine stays deterministic; the cost is a UI-layer mutation of the cached envelope, seeded in the first-frame cache block (4796-4802). This keeps evaluate_resign_offer pure and avoids touching the hot per-frame path.

FEATURE 3 COMPETING AI SUITORS + FEATURE 4 LIVE BIDDING (one suitor loop): Reuse B8's existing Candidate/Bid math (GameManager.cpp:2000-2092) verbatim but drive it from the OFFSEASON per-day loop (888-950) alongside run_offseason_fa_decay() (904). One new function `tick_fa_suitors(log)` iterates fa_suitors_: for each FA the user is courting (or any walked/pool FA), let up to 3 region/budget/need-filtered rivals place escalating bids across the 14 days; winner = max(willingness*amount_k) exactly as 2063; on deadline expiry the rival signs via Team::sign_player with gm.year (NOT year+1 — offseason convention, gui_main.cpp:5054) and explicit budget debit (poach pattern 2088). SuitorState holds {int deadline_day; std::vector<RivalBid> bids; int best_amount_k;}. Never-free safe: keyed by const Player*, cleared on new-world reset.

FEATURE 5 MULTI-YEAR STRUCTURE: Add `Promise promise{};` and a defaulted per-year array `std::array<int,5> yearly_amount_k{};` (or `int annual_raise_pct = 0;`) to Contract. exp_year stays the canonical live counter; amount_k stays year-1 salary so payroll (Team.cpp:717), salary_log (724), and valuation (809/998/2250) are untouched. The multi-year structure is READ-ONLY extra data those sites ignore. Set it at the SAME 3 canonical write sites (Team.cpp:442, 2347, Player.cpp:465) where exp_year is computed — colocated so the invariant exp_year=first_full_season+years-1 is preserved.

FEATURE 6 OVR+PERF+ACCOLADE VALUE (re-weight, not rebuild): gen_contract already has all 3 axes as multipliers (value_p=0.55*ovr+0.45*potential at Player.cpp:321; star_mult accolades 333-339; last_season_mult/trophy_mult perf+accolades 359-382). Minimal change = consolidate the double-counted accolade paths (career_mvps*4 AND awards.size()*5; star_mult avg_match_rating AND last_season history.back) by reading trophy_summary() once (Player.cpp:1007) and reconciling career-vs-recent form into a single accolade_mult. Preserve the per-band absolute_max caps (395-415) — they are the real ceiling. No signature change; the builder (Player.cpp:457-466) still returns Contract by value.

FEATURE 7 PERSONALITY WEIGHTING: No new scalar field needed — Desire enum + desire_salary_mult (Player.cpp:2533) + the archetype hard-gates in evaluate_resign_offer (2943-2968) already ARE the personality system. Minimal change: (a) remove the propose_resign_offer double-count (2642-2655 re-applies Loyal/RingChaser/Mercenary on top of desire_salary_mult already applied in gen_contract:440) so personality is weighted once; (b) make the context-free seed path (ctor 132-133, current_year=0, no team_ctx) carry a neutral personality factor. If a finer 'ambition' axis is wanted later, add `int ambition = 50;` defaulted on Player following the roll-once-at-spawn convention — but the minimal build ships on Desire alone.
data_model: struct Contract (Player.h:246-249): keep `int amount_k=25;` and `int exp_year=0;` FIRST and untouched (protects the 3 canonical formula sites + smoke_test.cpp:1281). Add AFTER them, each with a default member initializer to retain aggregate-ness: `int signing_bonus_k = 0;`, `std::array<int,5> yearly_amount_k{};` (or `int annual_raise_pct = 0;`), and `Promise promise{};`. Declare `struct Promise { bool guaranteed_starter = false; Role promised_role = Role::Flex; int no_bench_years = 0; };` as a nested aggregate ABOVE its use (all members default-initialized -> Contract stays a C++17 aggregate). Verified: ZERO positional `Contract{a,b}` call sites exist, every construction is `Contract c;`+assignment or `contract{}`, so new defaulted fields are fully backward-safe.

ResignBreakdown (Player.h:628-644): add `int counter_offer_k = 0;` and `int reject_round_penalty = 0;` (both defaulted). All 3 UI consumers (gui_main.cpp:4943,6087,7903) and accepts_resign_offer read existing fields and keep compiling. If a counter component is added into desire_mod, also push a matching entry into the existing `labels` vector so the displayed factors still sum to total (per the desire_mod/role_mod split trap at 3052-3057).

ResignOffer (Player.h:590-597): OPTIONAL additive `double rival_interest = 0.0; int deadline_day = 0;` (defaulted) to carry live-suitor signal into the modal's LEFT column (insert UI around 4888-4893). Seed in the first-frame cache block (4796-4802); these are populated from fa_suitors_ for FA mode, left 0 for Resign mode.

GameManager (mirror fa_demand_baseline_k_): add `std::unordered_map<const Player*, SuitorState> fa_suitors_;` declared/cleared at the SAME lines as the baseline map (285, 2324) so new-world reset already clears it. `struct SuitorState { int deadline_day; int best_amount_k; std::vector<RivalBid> bids; };` and `struct RivalBid { TeamPtr team; int amount_k; int years; };` — TeamPtr/PlayerPtr are shared_ptr held without ever deleting (never-free invariant).

AppState (gui_main.cpp:1343-1371): add session-only fields mirroring the existing resign_/fa_ pattern: `int *_bonus_choice = 0;`, `bool *_role_promise = false;`, `int *_reject_rounds = 0;`. New ref params threaded into DrawNegotiationModal (caller-owns-state contract, 4759-4766) — no statics. (Re)seed in the `if (cached_for != p.get())` block (4796-4802); clean up in the close handler (5095-5099) to avoid leaking across players.

KEEP-CALL-SITES-COMPILING STRATEGY: (1) every new field has a default member initializer; (2) no existing field is reordered, renamed, or retyped; (3) salary_log std::pair<int,int> (Team.cpp:724) is NOT widened — signing-bonus history, if needed, goes in a NEW parallel vector/struct, never by editing the pair; (4) gen_contract/evaluate_resign_offer/propose_resign_offer signatures are unchanged — new behavior rides inside their existing bodies; (5) signing-time writes (bonus/promise/multi-year) happen ONLY at the 3 canonical exp_year sites (Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347, Player.cpp:465) plus the gen_contract builder (Player.cpp:457-466).
pros: Lowest blast radius: all 7 features ship without changing a single existing function signature or aggregate-init site — adds defaulted fields, one bisection in an already-monotonic scorer, one suitor loop reusing B8's proven math, and UI in one shared modal helper.

Reuses verified invariants instead of fighting them: B8's willingness*amount_k bidding (2063), the deterministic gen_contract(randomize=false) ask, the monotonic evaluate scorer, and the never-free fa_demand_baseline_k_ pattern all transfer directly.
Counter-offer needs NO new engine math — it bisects the existing pure scorer, so it is automatically deterministic and consistent with the live breakdown the slider already shows.
Personality and accolade features are re-weightings of existing multiplier chains, not new subsystems — preserves the 2026-05 salary bands and keeps smoke tests #12/#13 in range.
Single edit point per concern: DrawNegotiationModal for all UI, the 3 canonical write sites for all signing-time data, gen_contract for all valuation — easy to review and revert.
cons: Reject-cost lives at the UI/caller layer (mutating cached envelope) rather than in the engine, so an AI-vs-AI re-sign path (GameManager.cpp:1913-1959) wouldn't feel the same escalating cost unless mirrored into SuitorState — two cost code paths to keep aligned.

Multi-year structure is stored-but-mostly-ignored: payroll/valuation still key off amount_k (year-1), so true per-year cap accounting is deferred — yearly_amount_k is descriptive data until those sites opt in.

The pre-existing propose vs evaluate ask DRIFT (randomized vs deterministic gen_contract, current_year actual vs 0) is only partially addressed; the minimal build de-double-counts personality but does not unify the two ask derivations into one source of truth.
Promise/role-clause has no enforcement teeth in the sim loop (no mid-contract benching penalty) — it is a negotiation-time lever and a stored flag, not a season-long constraint, in this minimal cut.
Live suitors only run during the 14 offseason days; in-season FA contention is out of scope, matching B8's once-at-year-end limitation rather than removing it.
architectures.risk_to_compile: "Very low. Risks are confined and known: (1) Contract must remain a C++17 aggregate — guaranteed because Promise and all new fields use default member initializers and no user-declared constructor; verified there are ZERO positional `Contract{a,b}` sites so defaulted additions cannot break init. (2) Role::Flex must be a valid default for Promise.promised_role — confirm the Role enum value name. (3) New ResignBreakdown/ResignOffer fields are defaulted so the 3 UI consumers and accepts_resign_offer compile untouched. (4) The counter-offer bisection must stay rng-free to honor the every-frame determinism constraint (Player.cpp:2811-2821). (5) fa_suitors_ must be cleared at the same reset point as fa_demand_baseline_k_ (GameManager.cpp:285,2324) or per-player state leaks across worlds. (6) Offseason-day suitor signings must pass gm.year, not year+1 (gui_main.cpp:5054 convention), and must explicitly debit budget (sign_player does not). No existing signature changes, so the large call-site surface compiles unmodified."

architectures.angle: FULL NEGOTIATION STATE MACHINE: an explicit, GUI-driven NegotiationState object that owns the entire courting session (rounds, pending terms, suitor bids, deadline) and treats every existing Player engine call (gen_contract / propose_resign_offer / evaluate_resign_offer) as a stateless evaluator the machine drives. The state machine is the new source of truth for "what is on the table right now"; the player engine stays the source of truth for "would they accept THIS". This is the most professional/flexible angle because all 7 features become either fields on the state object or one explicit transition function, and nothing about acceptance math changes — we only wrap it.
architecture: CORE OBJECT: add a new header struct `vlr::NegotiationState` (own file negotiation.h, or appended after ResignOffer in Player.h) that is pure data + a few pure transition helpers. It is owned by the GUI layer (AppState), NOT by Player/Team — so it never touches the never-free invariant and is rebuilt per courting session. Fields: `const Player* target=nullptr; std::string team_name; int round=0; int max_rounds=4; int deadline_year=0; int deadline_day=0; ContractTerms pending{}; ContractTerms last_player_counter{}; std::vector<SuitorBid> suitors; double reject_heat=0.0; NegPhase phase=NegPhase::Opening;` plus a cached deterministic `ResignOffer baseline` (the player ask, computed ONCE at session start to kill the propose/evaluate drift the memory flags).

PHASES (the actual state machine): `enum class NegPhase { Opening, AwaitingPlayer, PlayerCountered, Accepted, RejectedHard, Expired, LostToSuitor }`. GUI drives transitions only through ONE function `NegotiationState::submit_offer(const Player&, const Team&, const ContractTerms& offer)` which: (1) increments round; (2) calls the EXISTING `evaluate_resign_offer(offer.amount_k, offer.years, team, offer.role)` — unchanged engine; (3) if will_accept -> phase=Accepted; (4) else compute a deterministic counter via monotonic bisection on amount (engine is documented monotonic in amount_k, Player.h:621) to find the smallest amount that flips will_accept, store it in last_player_counter, phase=PlayerCountered; (5) apply reject cost; (6) if round>max_rounds or deadline passed -> Expired. Commit (resign_player/sign_player) only happens when phase==Accepted and the user presses Send — the machine never signs by itself.

FEATURE 1 COUNTER-OFFERS: no engine change. submit_offer bisects amount_choice in [offer.min_acceptable_k .. kSalaryCapK] each rejection (and tests years-1 / years+1 within [1,max_acceptable_years]) using evaluate_resign_offer purely, returning the minimal accepting ContractTerms into last_player_counter. GUI renders it as a 'Player would accept: $X/yr x Y' card in the right column of DrawNegotiationModal after line 4977. Deterministic because the baseline ask is cached (no randomize_amount).

FEATURE 2 REJECT COST: `reject_heat` accumulates on every rejected submit_offer (e.g. += 0.18 per round). It feeds TWO real effects without breaking monotonicity-in-amount: (a) it decrements `max_rounds`-equivalent patience and tightens the deadline; (b) at COMMIT time, an accepted deal pays a one-time `signing_bonus_k` surcharge proportional to reject_heat (lowballing made them want guaranteed money up front), wired through the new Contract field. It MUST NOT subtract from salary_mod inside evaluate (that would violate the load-bearing 'higher offer never scores lower' invariant in Player.h:621-627). Cost is modeled as patience/deadline/bonus, all OUTSIDE the score function.

FEATURE 3 COMPETING AI SUITORS: `std::vector<SuitorBid> suitors` where `struct SuitorBid { std::string team_name; int amount_k; int years; double composite; };`. Seeded at session start by reusing the EXACT B8 candidate-collection logic (GameManager.cpp:2000-2043): same-region, budget-floor = market_value_estimate*1.1, positional-need filter, ranked by team_import_appetite. Each suitor's bid amount comes from gen_contract(team_ctx) + propose_resign_offer willingness, composite = willingness*amount_k (the existing GameManager.cpp:2063 winner metric). GUI shows 'Other interest: N teams' in the LEFT asking column (~gui_main.cpp:4888).

FEATURE 4 LIVE AI BIDDING: the suitors escalate over the OFFSEASON 14-day loop. Add a per-day tick `NegotiationState::advance_day()` called from the existing offseason hook (GameManager.cpp:888-950, beside run_offseason_fa_decay) that bumps each suitor's amount_k toward their max budget and decrements days-to-deadline. If deadline passes with phase!=Accepted and the best suitor's composite beats the user's pending composite, phase=LostToSuitor and the rival signs via the SAME Team::sign_player path (using gm.year per the OFFSEASON contract-year convention, NOT year+1). This is the user-facing inverse of B8: rivals act on a pool FA the user is courting.

FEATURE 5 MULTI-YEAR STRUCTURE: replace the scalar offer with `struct ContractTerms { int amount_k=25; int years=1; int signing_bonus_k=0; Role role=Role::Count(sentinel->primary); std::array<int,5> per_year_k{}; bool starter_promise=false; };`. per_year_k holds an optional salary ramp; when all zero the deal is flat at amount_k (back-compat). evaluate_resign_offer still receives the headline amount_k+years, so the engine is untouched; the ramp is presentation+payroll only. years still flows through the canonical exp_year = first_full_season+years-1 formula at the 3 unchanged write sites.

FEATURE 6 OVR+PERFORMANCE+ACCOLADES VALUE: NOT a new path — re-weight inside the existing gen_contract multiplier chain (Player.cpp:417-441), the single ask engine both propose and evaluate call. Consolidate the double-counted accolade inputs (career_mvps*4 AND awards.size()*5 in star_mult, plus the recent-trophy string scan) onto trophy_summary() (Player.cpp:1007) and reconcile the form double-weight (avg_match_rating in star_mult vs history.back().rating in last_season_mult). Keep outputs inside the per-band absolute_max caps so smoke tests #12/#13 stay in range. The state machine just consumes the resulting baseline ask.

FEATURE 7 PERSONALITY WEIGHTING: keep using the categorical Desire enum (the only personality system that exists — no ambition/ego scalars today). Consolidate the desire double-count (desire_salary_mult inside gen_contract AND the Loyal/RingChaser/Mercenary re-multiply in propose_resign_offer 2642-2655) into ONE path. The state machine reads personality through three already-existing channels in evaluate: desire_mod archetype gates, refuses_to_negotiate mood floor (drives reject_heat and the dignity-floor counter), and desire_length_pref (years envelope). Mercenary etc. naturally shorten max_rounds/raise reject_heat via these gates — no new fields needed.

GUI INTEGRATION: a single new AppState member `NegotiationState neg;` (mirror the existing resign_*/fa_* caching fields at gui_main.cpp:1343-1371). DrawNegotiationModal (gui_main.cpp:4769, the ONE shared helper) seeds neg in the first-frame cache block (4796-4802, keyed on target.get()), reads neg.suitors/neg.round/neg.deadline for new UI, and on Send routes through neg.submit_offer instead of calling evaluate inline. Both Resign and FreeAgentSign modes inherit it. State cleared in the close handler (4795-4799/5095-5099).

SINGLE-SOURCE-OF-TRUTH FIX (bonus, addresses a real memory-flagged drift): NegotiationState.baseline is computed ONCE via propose_resign_offer at session open and reused; submit_offer's bisection uses evaluate_resign_offer (deterministic, randomize=false) against that frozen baseline, so the displayed ask, the player counter, and the accept decision can never disagree mid-session — closing the propose-vs-evaluate divergence the audit notes (randomized vs deterministic ask differing ±10-20%).
data_model: Contract struct (Player.h:246-249) — ADD three fields AFTER the existing two, each with a default member initializer so the aggregate stays an aggregate and value-init `contract{}` / `Contract c;` keep compiling (zero positional brace-inits exist in the codebase, confirmed): `int signing_bonus_k = 0;` `bool starter_promise = false;` `std::array<int,5> per_year_k{};`. amount_k and exp_year stay FIRST and untouched so the canonical exp_year formula sites (Player.cpp:465, Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347) and smoke_test.cpp:1281 need no edits.

NEW struct `ContractTerms` (UI/state-machine-side, NOT on Contract) in negotiation.h or near gui — the editable bundle the GUI sliders write: amount_k, years, signing_bonus_k, role, per_year_k, starter_promise. It is converted to a Contract only at commit. Keeping it separate from Contract avoids touching gen_contract's by-value builder for transient UI state.

NEW struct `NegotiationState` + `SuitorBid` + `enum class NegPhase` — pure data, GUI-owned, in AppState. No Player/Team member, so never-free invariant is untouched and no engine header gains a dependency on it. Holds raw `const Player*` / team_name strings (mirroring fa_demand_baseline_k_ keyed by const Player*), cleared on new-world reset.

sign_player / resign_player (Team.h:277-299) — KEEP existing overloads compiling by adding ONE new overload that takes a `const Contract& terms` (or defaulted `int signing_bonus_k = 0, bool starter_promise = false` trailing args on the role-aware sign_player at Team.h:284 and resign_player at Team.h:299). All current call sites (GameManager.cpp:2092, gui_main.cpp:5056/7980, resign paths) pass nothing new and get defaults. New bonus/promise written at the SAME 3 canonical signing sites the memory flags (Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347, and gen_contract seed Player.cpp:458-466).

gen_contract builder (Player.cpp:457-466) — leave the returned Contract's new fields at their defaults (signing_bonus_k=0, per_year_k zeroed, starter_promise=false). The ctor seed path (Player.cpp:132-133, current_year=0) stays sane because every new field defaults benignly before the player is signed.

salary_log (Player.h:401, vector<pair<int,int>>) — DO NOT widen the pair (Team.cpp:724 is positionally fixed and would break). If signing bonus needs historical logging, add a PARALLEL `std::vector<std::pair<int,int>> bonus_log;` member rather than editing the existing pair element types.

evaluate_resign_offer / ResignBreakdown — NO signature change required for the state machine itself (it bisects using the existing 4-arg overload). If reject_heat/bonus should be SHOWN in the breakdown, extend ResignBreakdown with a defaulted `int bonus_mod = 0;` and a matching labels entry (the UI sums labels, so an unlabeled mod would desync — Player.cpp:3052-3057 pattern). Prefer keeping cost OUT of the score (patience/deadline/bonus) to preserve the amount-monotonicity invariant.
pros: Maximally flexible: every one of the 7 features is either a field on NegotiationState or one branch of the single submit_offer/advance_day transition — adding an 8th (e.g. trade-clause, NTC) is additive, not a rewrite.

Zero risk to the acceptance engine: evaluate_resign_offer / gen_contract are consumed read-only and unchanged, so the load-bearing monotonicity invariant (Player.h:621-627) and all smoke-test salary bands are preserved by construction.

Fixes a real existing bug for free: caching one deterministic baseline ask per session eliminates the documented propose-vs-evaluate ±10-20% drift the audit flagged, so the displayed ask, the counter card, and the accept decision finally agree.
Counter-offers and the 'minimum accepting offer' card fall out of the documented amount-monotonicity via a 3-4 step bisection — no new heuristic, fully deterministic, no slider oscillation.

Reuses B8's existing candidate/budget-floor/positional-need collection and the willingness*amount_k winner metric (GameManager.cpp:2063) for suitors, so AI bidding behaves consistently with year-end market competition the user already sees.
GUI-owned state means no new lifetime obligations: NegotiationState holds raw Player*/team-name and is rebuilt per session, never freeing anything — the never-free invariant is untouched.
Single edit point: DrawNegotiationModal (gui_main.cpp:4769) is the one shared helper, so Re-sign and FreeAgentSign both inherit all 7 features from one change.
Back-compat by defaulted fields/args: existing Contract aggregate-inits, sign_player/resign_player call sites, and the 3 canonical exp_year write sites all keep compiling with no edits.
cons: Most code surface of the candidate angles: introduces a new struct family (NegotiationState/ContractTerms/SuitorBid/NegPhase) plus a per-day offseason tick and AppState wiring — more to get right than a thin in-modal counter card.

Reject-cost must be modeled OUTSIDE the score (patience/deadline/signing_bonus) to avoid breaking amount-monotonicity; designers wanting 'lowballing lowers their willingness directly' have to accept the indirection, which is less intuitive.

Live suitor bidding spans the 14-day OFFSEASON loop, so it only fully expresses during offseason; an in-season re-sign session gets suitors/deadline as a compressed/synthetic countdown, a slight behavioral asymmetry to document.

Personality stays categorical (Desire enum) — true per-player 'ambition/ego' weighting would still require new scalar fields this design deliberately defers, so personality depth is bounded by the 6 archetypes.

State seeding/cleanup has a known footgun: any new cached neg field must be (re)seeded in the cached_for!=p.get() block (gui_main.cpp:4796) AND cleared on close (5095) or it leaks across players — easy to forget.
Two suitor code paths now exist (year-end B8 and the new live offseason tick); they must be kept consistent or behavior drifts, adding maintenance coupling.
architectures.risk_to_compile: LOW. The acceptance engine (evaluate_resign_offer, gen_contract) and the 3 canonical exp_year write sites are consumed read-only and never edited, so smoke_test.cpp:1281 and the salary-band asserts (smoke_test.cpp:220-221) stay green. New Contract fields all carry default member initializers and go AFTER amount_k/exp_year, so the aggregate stays an aggregate and every `Contract c;`/`contract{}`/aggregate-init site compiles unchanged (grep confirms zero positional Contract{a,b} constructions). sign_player/resign_player keep their current overloads and gain bonus/promise only via trailing defaulted args, so GameManager.cpp:2092 and gui_main.cpp:5056/7980 compile untouched. salary_log's fixed pair (Team.cpp:724) is NOT modified — bonus logging goes to a new parallel vector. The only files that MUST change are gui_main.cpp (the single DrawNegotiationModal helper + AppState fields) and one offseason hook in GameManager.cpp; both are additive. Residual risk is confined to the new NegotiationState wiring, not the engine.

architectures.angle: MARKET-DRIVEN (Football-Manager style). Every expiring/FA player runs a per-player MARKET keyed by const Player* in a GameManager-owned map (mirroring fa_demand_baseline_k_). The market holds: a deterministic value estimate (gen_contract no-noise), a roster of competing AI suitors with live escalating bids resolved one per offseason day across the existing 14-day OFFSEASON loop, and the user as exactly one bidder. The player is the auctioneer: each day every standing offer (user's + each AI suitor's) is scored by the SAME evaluate_resign_offer engine, ranked by a market-utility score = f(salary, role_fit, ambition/window, loyalty), and the best STANDING offer wins only when the deadline expires — so the user can be outbid in real time. Counter-offers, reject-cost, multi-year structure, holistic value and personality weighting are all expressed as inputs/outputs of that one daily auction tick, not as a separate UI-only path.
architecture: CORE OBJECT — PlayerMarket (new struct, GameManager-owned, keyed by const Player*): { int value_k; deterministic market value from gen_contract(year, randomize_amount=false, randomize_exp=false, nullptr-or-neutral). int deadline_day; (offseason day the auction closes, e.g. seeded to day_in_phase+3..8). std::vector<MarketBid> bids; (one per active suitor incl. user). int rounds_elapsed; }. Stored as std::unordered_map<const Player*, PlayerMarket> active_markets_, declared/cleared next to fa_demand_baseline_k_ (GameManager.cpp:285,2324) and reset on new-world. Never-free safe because it keys raw const Player* into the perpetual PlayerPtr pool.

MarketBid (new struct): { TeamPtr team; bool is_user; int amount_k; int years; Role offered_role; int signing_bonus_k; bool starter_promise; int last_revised_day; }. This is the persistent, revisable offer — the user's Send-Offer writes/updates THEIR MarketBid instead of instantly signing; AI suitors append/revise theirs each day.

FEATURE 3+4 (competing AI suitors + live bidding) — reuse B8 verbatim as the suitor SEEDING pass. The B8 Candidate collection (region/budget-floor est*1.1/positional-need filter, GameManager.cpp:2007-2027) becomes market_seed_suitors(player): it populates PlayerMarket.bids with the same up-to-3 rival teams, each making an initial gen_contract+decide_contract_years bid. Difference from B8: instead of resolving once, the bids PERSIST in active_markets_.

FEATURE 3+4 daily tick — new market_tick_day() called from the OFFSEASON day loop (GameManager.cpp:888-950) right beside run_offseason_fa_decay()(904). Each day, for each active market: (a) let 0-1 AI suitors ESCALATE (raise amount toward value_k*overpay or add bonus) — escalation is the 'live bidding'; (b) re-rank all standing bids via the player's auctioneer score (below); (c) if today==deadline_day, AWARD to the top standing bid using the existing accepts_resign_offer final gate, then sign_player(...,gm.year) + explicit budget debit + poach/sign news, and erase the market. This spreads B8's instantaneous resolution across 14 days so the user can react.

AUCTIONEER SCORE (single source of truth) — the ranking that picks the winner is evaluate_resign_offer(bid.amount_k, bid.years, *bid.team, bid.offered_role).total, NOT a new formula. This automatically folds salary (monotonic salary_mod), window/contender (contender_mod), loyalty/tenure (loyalty_mod), years structure (years_mod), and personality archetype gates (desire_mod). Tie-break / soft ranking multiplies total by willingness from propose_resign_offer (reusing B8's comp = willingness*amount intuition). So 'player picks best by salary x role x ambition x loyalty' = argmax of evaluate_resign_offer.total over standing bids.

FEATURE 1 (counter-offers) — surfaced from the auctioneer, computed deterministically. Add Player::market_counter(int offered_amount, int years, const Team&, Role) returning a CounterOffer { int suggested_amount_k; int suggested_years; std::string ask; } that bisects amount in [min_acceptable_k .. value_k*overpay] to find the smallest amount where evaluate_resign_offer.total>=kResignAcceptThreshold (monotonic in amount, Player.h:621, so bisection is valid and deterministic). When the user's standing bid is currently NOT the top bid OR is below threshold, the modal shows 'They want at least $X for N years (currently leading: <Team>)'. No new scoring path — just an inverse query over the existing monotone score.

FEATURE 2 (reject cost) — make Send-Offer mutate market state instead of zero-cost retry. Each rejected/under-market user revision: bump rounds_elapsed and apply a SOFT patience penalty via the existing mood channel — bump_mood(team_name, +delta) on lowball (raises dignity floor 30+70*mood, Player.cpp:482) and optionally shorten deadline_day by 1. This reuses the ONLY mutable emotional state (mood) and the existing refuses_to_negotiate path; the cost is 'lowballing makes them harder to sign and burns days', enforced because the deadline and rival escalation are now real.

FEATURE 5 (multi-year structure) — already carried: bid.years feeds evaluate_resign_offer years_mod and the sign path. Add structure depth via Contract.signing_bonus_k (new field) and a Contract.promise sub-struct (starter_promise) so multi-year deals can differ by guarantees, scored as a small additive bonus in evaluate_resign_offer.

FEATURE 6 (OVR+performance+accolades value) — value_k is gen_contract output, which already weights OVR+potential (value_p), career-phase, star_mult (career_mvps/awards/avg_match_rating), last_season form, trophy_mult, role_salary_mult. No new valuation engine; the market's value estimate IS gen_contract. Optionally consolidate the double-counted form/accolade multipliers using trophy_summary() but that is a re-weighting, not structural.

FEATURE 7 (personality weighting) — desire archetype already enters gen_contract (desire_salary_mult) and evaluate_resign_offer (desire_mod gates). To make 'ambition' first-class, add ONE scalar Player::ambition (1..99, rolled once at spawn like desire) read in two spots: (a) auctioneer score weights window/contender harder for high-ambition (bias toward winning teams over money), (b) min_acceptable_k floor_frac raised for high-ego. This makes the player pick 'salary x role x ambition x loyalty' truly multi-axis.

USER-AS-ONE-BIDDER — the FA Sign modal and Re-sign modal both write through to the player's MarketBid via the existing DrawNegotiationModal references; Send Offer no longer signs instantly, it registers/updates the user's standing bid and shows live rival standings + deadline pulled from active_markets_[target]. Re-sign is the same market with the incumbent team holding a loyalty-boosted seed bid.

Existing `Contract c;`/`contract{}` and the 3 canonical exp_year write sites (Player.cpp:465, Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347) keep compiling untouched; new fields default. gen_contract builder (Player.cpp:458-466) leaves them default unless a signing site sets them.

gen_contract overloads (Player.h:559-567) — UNCHANGED signatures; both keep their defaulted args (current_year, randomize_amount=true, randomize_exp=false, team_ctx=nullptr). Every existing sign/resign/seed call site compiles as-is.

salary_log (Player.h:401, vector<pair<int,int>>) — DO NOT change element type (Team.cpp:724 is positional). If signing bonus needs history, add a parallel vector or widen via a new struct, never edit the pair.
pros: Single source of truth for acceptance AND ranking: the winner of the auction is argmax of the same evaluate_resign_offer the UI breakdown shows, so the user's live breakdown never disagrees with who actually signs — eliminates the propose/evaluate drift risk the audit flagged.

Maximal code reuse: B8 (GameManager.cpp:1979-2105) is the suitor/bid engine, run_offseason_fa_decay is the per-day hook pattern, fa_demand_baseline_k_ is the per-Player offseason-state pattern, mood is the patience/reject-cost channel. Little net-new math.

All 7 features fall out of ONE mechanism (the daily auction over standing bids) rather than 7 bolt-ons; counter-offer is an inverse query over the existing monotone score, reject-cost is mood mutation, competing/live bidding is B8 persisted across 14 days.

Backward-compatible by construction: every Contract/ResignOffer addition is a defaulted member on a confirmed aggregate; every method signature is preserved or extended with trailing defaults; the 3 canonical exp_year write sites stay untouched.

Genuine FM-style tension: the user is one bidder, can be outbid live, faces a real deadline because rival escalation + deadline_day are now enforced engine state — fixing the current zero-cost-retry, no-contention FA flow.
Personality becomes a real axis via a single ambition scalar plus the existing desire gates, giving salary x role x ambition x loyalty without ripping out the categorical Desire system.
cons: Behavioral change to the user FA flow: Send Offer no longer signs instantly — it registers a standing bid and may lose to a rival at deadline. This changes UX expectations and needs clear modal messaging (deadline, current leader) or users will think the button is broken.

evaluate_resign_offer is now called as a RANKING function across all standing bids every offseason day for every active market — it must stay deterministic and cheap; fine today (no rng, O(roster)) but the per-day loop adds O(markets * suitors) eval calls.

Spreading resolution across 14 days introduces ordering/timing edge cases the one-shot B8 never had: a market whose deadline lands after the offseason loop ends, or a pool FA consumed by ai_full_offseason_pass before its market resolves — needs a fallback to force-resolve any open market before phase wrap (GameManager.cpp:941) and before cleanup.

Signing-bonus scoring risks bypassing the per-band salary cap (kSalaryCapK / per-band absolute_max) unless bonus is treated as a separate accounting line, not folded into amount_k — must debit budget for bonus explicitly or budget leaks.

Adding ambition is a real balance surface; if it shifts min_acceptable_k floor_frac it must be mirrored in BOTH propose_resign_offer and evaluate_resign_offer or they diverge further (the exact drift the audit warned about).

Mood-as-reject-cost reuses a channel that also feeds amount_with_mood (+20% ask) and the dignity floor — lowball penalties could double-count into the ask; must audit amount_with_mood / refuses_to_negotiate interaction to avoid runaway demands.

architectures.angle: PERSONALITY-FIRST: a value+behavior core where OVR/performance/accolades produce a BASE VALUE (the deterministic ask engine), and three new persisted scalar traits — ambition, loyalty, greed (0..99, rolled once at spawn like Desire) — modulate every negotiation behavior: demand size, counter-offer aggressiveness, suitor-switching, live-bid willingness, and reject sensitivity. The Desire enum stays as a coarse archetype that SEEDS the three scalars, so existing desire_* gates keep firing while the scalars give continuous, per-player variation. Negotiation mechanics (counter-offers, reject cost, suitors, bidding, multi-year) are thin wrappers that read the same trait+value core, never re-deriving it independently.
architecture: LAYER 0 - VALUE CORE (unchanged math, single source): gen_contract() (Player.cpp:301-467) remains THE base-value engine. value_p=0.55*ovr()+0.45*potential plus the multiplier chain (phase/star/lead/upside/risk/last_season/trophy/veteran/role) already folds OVR+performance+accolades. Feature 6 (OVR+perf+accolades value) = this, kept intact so smoke tests #12/#13 salary bands stay in range. Reconcile the existing double-counts noted in memory (form via avg_match_rating AND history.back().rating; accolades via career_mvps+awards.size() AND trophy scan) by keying accolade weight off trophy_summary() once, but that is a re-weighting, not a structural change.

LAYER 1 - PERSONALITY TRAITS (the new core): add three int scalars to Player: ambition, loyalty, greed (each 1..99). Rolled ONCE in generate_player/ctor via a new pick_spawn_traits(Desire) that seeds them from the existing Desire archetype (Greedy->high greed; Loyal->high loyalty; RingChaser/Competitor->high ambition; Mercenary->high greed+low loyalty; StabilitySeeker->low ambition+high loyalty) plus jitter. This makes personality CENTRAL and continuous while preserving the Desire enum so all desire_* gates (Player.cpp:2533/2568/2613/2943-2968) keep compiling and firing. A new trait_salary_mult(team) and trait_floor_frac()/trait_reject_sensitivity() expose them.

LAYER 2 - DEMAND FORMATION: personality modulates the BASE VALUE into a DEMAND. gen_contract's desire_salary_mult (Player.cpp:440) is widened to fold (1 + greed_bias) where greed raises the ask above market and ambition adds a premium vs non-contenders. propose_resign_offer's second desire layer (Player.cpp:2642-2655) is consolidated to remove the documented double-count and instead apply ONE trait-driven adjustment. floor_frac/min_acceptable_k (Player.cpp:2751-2757) becomes trait-driven: high greed/ambition -> higher floor_frac (0.90-0.95), loyal-on-same-team -> lower (0.80). This is the single demand path both propose_ and evaluate_ must read.

LAYER 3 - EVALUATE / REJECT SENSITIVITY (Feature 2 reject cost, Feature 7 personality weighting): evaluate_resign_offer (Player.cpp:2807) keeps its additive score-vs-threshold(55) core and the monotonic-in-amount invariant (Player.h:621-627). Personality enters desire_mod (the existing extension point Player.cpp:2943-2977): greed sharpens the below-ask penalty slope, loyalty boosts loyalty_mod on same_team, ambition sharpens the contender/strategy penalty. NEW reject-cost state: a per-(Player,team) negotiation_patience counter on a new NegotiationState struct (see data_model). Each rejected Send Offer decrements patience scaled by reject_sensitivity = f(greed, ambition); when patience hits 0 the player hard-blocks further offers this window (mirrors the hard_block branch Player.cpp:3016-3028) and bumps mood DOWN. Determinism preserved: patience is read-only inside evaluate (passed in), mutated only on actual Send (gui_main.cpp:5017/5047), so the every-frame live breakdown never jitters.

LAYER 4 - COUNTER-OFFER (Feature 1): evaluate_resign_offer is monotonic in amount, so the smallest accepting salary is found by bisecting amt in [min_acceptable_k, cap] each frame (deterministic, no rng). Add counter_amount_k and counter_years to ResignBreakdown (defaulted, so all 3 UI consumers + accepts_resign_offer compile unchanged). The player 'counters' by surfacing min_acceptable_k / max_acceptable_years / the bisected accept-price. Greed/ambition set how aggressively the counter sits above min_acceptable_k (greedy counters near full ask; loyal-same-team counters near floor). UI renders a counter card in the reject branch (gui_main.cpp after 4977 / reject branches 5017,5047).

LAYER 5 - COMPETING AI SUITORS + LIVE BIDDING (Features 3,4): reuse B8 (GameManager.cpp:1979-2105) Candidate/Bid math (composite = willingness*amount_k, accepts_resign_offer final gate). Promote it from a once-at-year-end pass to a per-offseason-day tick in the OFFSEASON loop (GameManager.cpp:888-950) alongside run_offseason_fa_decay. Add a GameManager::fa_suitors_ map keyed by const Player* (mirror fa_demand_baseline_k_ at GameManager.cpp:285/2324, cleared on world reset) holding {vector<TeamPtr> suitors, int deadline_day, int top_bid_k}. Each offseason day, for any FA the user is courting (or ovr-gated walked stars), 1-3 region/budget/need-filtered rivals escalate bids; ambition makes the player MORE likely to entertain/switch to a higher external bid (suitor-switching = ambition raises the willingness weight on rival composite vs incumbent), loyalty resists switching. On deadline a rival signs via Team::sign_player using gm.year (offseason convention) + manual budget debit. Never-free invariant held: only TeamPtr/Player* held, release just flips team_name.

LAYER 6 - MULTI-YEAR STRUCTURE (Feature 5): contract length already flows through desire_length_pref->years buckets (propose 2665, evaluate 2826) and Team::decide_contract_years. Personality refines it: ambition shortens preferred length on non-contenders (bet-on-self), loyalty lengthens on same_team, greed is length-neutral but Mercenary-style long-deal penalties (years_mod, Player.cpp:2925-2934) get sharpened by low loyalty. Add optional signing_bonus_k to Contract (defaulted 0) and an optional starter/role-promise to support richer multi-year deals; both set at the 3 canonical exp_year write sites so the canonical formula stays untouched. Factor the duplicated year-bucketing (propose 2665-2669 / evaluate 2826-2830) into one helper to prevent drift.

CROSS-CUTTING SINGLE-SOURCE FIX: the memory's documented drift bug (propose_ and evaluate_ each independently call gen_contract with different randomize/current_year and re-derive min_acceptable_k) is resolved by routing both through one deterministic ask helper (gen_contract(randomize_amount=false) as evaluate already does) and one year-bucket helper and one trait_floor_frac(). propose_ keeps its randomized DISPLAY ask but its FLOOR is computed from the deterministic ask, so the UI floor and engine floor are identical numbers, not just identical formulas.

pros: Single source of truth for value AND personality: every feature reads gen_contract (value) + the three scalars (behavior), so counter-offers, suitor bids, and reject cost can never disagree with the displayed ask — directly fixes the documented propose_/evaluate_ drift bug.

Maximally compile-safe: every new Contract/ResignOffer/ResignBreakdown field has a default member initializer; every new function arg/overload is defaulted or delegates; grep-confirmed there are zero positional Contract brace-inits. All listed call sites (Team.cpp payroll/valuation/sign sites, smoke_test asserts, the 3 UI consumers, GameManager AI paths) compile untouched.

Personality is continuous, not just categorical: three 0-99 scalars give per-player variation while the Desire enum (which seeds them) keeps all existing desire_* gates and smoke-test behavior intact — additive, not a rewrite.

Reuses proven mechanics: B8's willingness*amount_k bidding and accepts_resign_offer gate already exist; the feature promotes them to a per-day offseason tick rather than inventing a new bidding engine. Counter-offer reuses the existing monotonic-in-amount invariant via bisection (deterministic).

Determinism preserved where it must be: evaluate_resign_offer stays rng-free and patience/suitor state is passed in read-only (defaulted nullptr), so the every-frame live breakdown and slider never jitter — honoring the load-bearing UI constraint.
Never-free invariant honored: all new offseason state is keyed by const Player*/TeamPtr, cleared only on world reset; no team/player is ever destroyed.

Mood is not double-counted: amount_with_mood and refuses_to_negotiate stay at the UI/negotiation layer; the new scalars feed gen_contract/demand and evaluate score, with the existing refuses_to_negotiate -22 soft hook left as the single mood channel.
cons: Three correlated scalars seeded from one Desire enum risk redundancy with the enum and with each other (ambition vs Competitor/RingChaser overlap). Needs a clear ownership rule: scalars own continuous magnitude, enum owns categorical hard-gates, or the two will fight.

Reject-cost / patience introduces MUTABLE per-negotiation state, breaking today's pure zero-cost-retry model. The mutation must be confined to the actual Send Offer call sites (gui_main 5017/5047) and the offseason tick; if any every-frame path mutates it, the breakdown desyncs.

Per-offseason-day live suitor bidding changes pacing semantics: B8 fires once at year-end today, so spreading bids across 14 days plus the existing year-end B8 + ai_full_offseason_pass fill risks double-signing or pool depletion ordering bugs (the fill already ran at year-end before the offseason days).

Re-weighting gen_contract to de-double-count form/accolades can shift salary bands; smoke tests #12/#13 and the 2026-05 rebalance ranges (elite 80-180K, mid 30-60K, bench 10-18K) must be re-validated.

signing_bonus_k can bypass kSalaryCapK in payroll/affordability math (Team.cpp:717 sum, gui cost = amt*1000) unless every cost site accounts for it; salary_log is a fixed pair<int,int> so bonus logging needs a parallel structure, not an edit to the pair.

Suitor-switching driven by ambition vs incumbent loyalty is a tuning-heavy balance problem; willingness is clamped 0.05-0.95 and same_team-biased, so rivals already need higher amount_k to win — getting switch frequency to feel right will need iteration.

More overloads/defaulted args increase the surface where a caller silently gets neutral defaults (e.g. a sign path that forgets to pass the negotiated bonus), which compiles but quietly drops the deal term.
architectures.risk_to_compile: LOW. Every structural change is purely additive and defaulted: (1) new Contract fields (signing_bonus_k, Promise promise{}) all have default member initializers and Contract stays a C++17 aggregate (grep confirmed ZERO positional `Contract{a,b}` call sites, so no brace-init breaks); (2) new Player scalars ambition/loyalty/greed default to 50, so every existing Player construction and the ctor gen_contract seed compile and behave neutrally; (3) ResignOffer/ResignBreakdown gain defaulted fields only — the 3 UI consumers (gui_main 4943/6087/7903) and accepts_resign_offer read existing fields, so they compile untouched; (4) the new evaluate_resign_offer NegotiationState* arg is defaulted nullptr and the existing 3-/4-arg overloads delegate to it, so the every-frame UI and AI accept paths are unchanged; (5) sign_player/resign_player gain trailing defaulted args, so GameManager (1959/2092) and gui_main (5056/7980) callers compile as-is. The only places that MUST be edited (not just compile) are the 3 canonical exp_year write sites (Player.cpp:465, Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347) IF bonus/promise are set at signing, and these edits sit beside — not on — the `exp_year = first_full_season + years - 1` lines so smoke_test.cpp:1281 stays green. Residual risk is behavioral (band re-validation, patience-mutation discipline, offseason pacing), not compile-time.

featureSpecs.feature: COUNTER-OFFERS for the re-sign / FA-sign negotiation modal: when the user's offer is a "near miss" (rejected but close — within a defined short band of acceptance), evaluate_resign_offer returns a concrete, deterministic counter (a minimum salary $K and/or a shorter term that would CLEAR acceptance, total>=55 with no hard-block). The modal renders it as an "Accept-counter" card with a one-click button that snaps the sliders to the counter values. Reject of a far-off offer still shows only the existing reason line (no counter). Never-free invariant untouched (read-only Player math, no allocations beyond the existing breakdown), and all existing call sites keep compiling because the new data rides on additive default-initialized fields of ResignBreakdown.
featureSpecs.formula_or_logic:
DETERMINISM (load-bearing): evaluate_resign_offer is called every UI frame and MUST stay rng-free (Player.cpp:2811-2821). featureSpecs.feature: REJECT COST: lowball/insulting re-sign or FA offers now (1) bump the player's per-team mood AGAINST the team, (2) raise the next deterministic min-ask via the existing mood->ask channel, and (3) decrement a per-(player,team) offers-remaining counter so the free reject-retry loop terminates. Never-free invariant untouched (no allocs/frees; mood map and a new counter map are value state on persistent PlayerPtrs). All existing evaluate_resign_offer / propose_resign_offer / sign_player / resign_player / accepts_resign_offer call sites keep compiling because new state is added as defaulted members + new optional helper methods, not signature changes to existing functions.
featureSpecs.formula_or_logic:
CLASSIFY THE REJECTED OFFER (run only when bd.will_accept == false, i.e. an actual reject was sent). Use the ratio r = amount_k / ro.amount_k where ro.amount_k is the DETERMINISTIC ask already computed inside evaluate_resign_offer (Player.cpp:2822, det.amount_k) and the verdict band already produced at Player.cpp:3033-3037. Severity tiers (drive both mood bump and offer-count cost):
  - INSULTING  : bd.total < 35  (verdict=="INSULTING")  OR r < 0.70  -> mood_delta = +0.18, offers_cost = 2
  - LOWBALL    : 35 <= bd.total < 55 AND r < 0.90        -> mood_delta = +0.08, offers_cost = 1
  - NEAR-MISS  : reject but r >= 0.90 (e.g. years-only / archetype-gate reject, offer was respectful) -> mood_delta = +0.00, offers_cost = 1
Mood is bumped via the EXISTING single choke point bump_mood(team_name, mood_delta) (Player.cpp:473), which clamps to [0,1]. Higher mood is "madder at this org": it already (a) raises the dignity floor refuses_to_negotiate => floor_k = 30+70*mood (Player.cpp:490), which feeds desire_mod -= 22 in evaluate (Player.cpp:2973-2977), and (b) raises the displayed/effective ask via amount_with_mood => base*(1+0.20*mood) (Player.cpp:479). So a +0.18 insult bump raises the dignity floor by ~+12.6K and the mood-adjusted ask by up to +3.6% per insult, both monotonic. NO new ask formula is invented — reusing the mood->ask path satisfies "raises their next min ask" with one number.

OFFERS-PER-TARGET LIMIT: kRejectOffersPerTarget = 3 (constexpr in Player.h near kSalaryFloorK usage, or file-local in gui_main.cpp). Per (player, team) the user gets 3 reject-eligible Send-Offer presses before the target is locked for the session. Counter starts at 3 and is decremented by offers_cost on every REJECTED send (accepted sends do not decrement and close the modal anyway). When remaining <= 0: the modal's Send Offer button is disabled and a banner "Player has walked away from talks (too many lowball offers)" shows; the FA can then be lost to the existing B8 / offseason rival-bid path with no further user retries. ACCEPT path never touches the counter.

PERSISTENCE: counter is per-(player,team) and per-negotiation-window, keyed by const Player* (mirroring GameManager::fa_demand_baseline_k_ at GameManager.cpp:285,2416). Mood persists on the Player (FreeAgentMood mood, Player.h:400) and decays via the EXISTING decay_mood(0.015) called each offseason day from run_offseason_fa_decay (GameManager.cpp:2441) and via the year-end pass — so reject anger fades across seasons automatically, no new decay code. The offers-remaining counter is SESSION/window state (cleared on new world reset alongside fa_demand_baseline_k_ at GameManager.cpp:285,2324, and re-seeded when the modal opens a new target).

  struct SuitorState { int interest_count=0; int deadline_day=0; int top_bid_k=0; TeamPtr top_bidder; int user_pending_k=0; int user_pending_yrs=0; bool user_courting=false; };
  std::unordered_map<Player*, SuitorState> fa_suitors_;

Re-stamp every frame the slider moves so user_pending_k tracks the live offer. On sign/walk call gm.clear_fa_courted(p.get()).

PER-DAY SUITOR TICK (run_offseason_suitor_tick, called at GameManager.cpp:904). For each region ladder FA (team_name=="Free Agent", !is_retired), only act if fa_suitors_[p].user_courting (rivals only contend for FAs the user is pursuing — keeps it cheap and on-screen):
  1. SEED ON FIRST SIGHT: deadline_day = day_in_phase + DEADLINE_DAYS(4) so the user gets ~4 of the 14 offseason days to close. interest_count seeded from candidate scan below.
  2. CANDIDATE SCAN (reuse B8 GameManager.cpp:2004-2034 verbatim, swap w.prev_team->region for p->region, swap w.prev_team exclusion for none): same region, != user_team, budget >= market_value_estimate(p)*1.1*1000, positional need target_comp.need[role] > have. interest_count = candidates.size() (cap 3 as B8 does at :2034). This is the "Other interest: N teams" number.
  3. ESCALATING BID (once per day, top candidate only): bid_k = round(gen_contract(year,true,false,cand.get()).amount_k * escalation), escalation ramps 1.00 -> 1.15 over the days remaining: escalation = 1.0 + 0.05 * (DEADLINE_DAYS - days_left). Skip if bid_k < propose_resign_offer(*cand,year).min_acceptable_k OR cand->budget < bid_k*1000. Keep the best across days in top_bid_k/top_bidder (max comp = willingness*bid_k, exactly B8 GameManager.cpp:2063).
  4. RESOLUTION — "rival beats user pending interest": rival wins iff top_bid_k > user_pending_k (the live slider offer) AND day_in_phase >= deadline_day. On win, sign elsewhere via the B8 poach pipeline (GameManager.cpp:2075-2104): accepts_resign_offer gate, release (no prev team — already FA, skip release), debit top_bidder->budget -= top_bid_k*1000, sign_player(p, top_bid_yrs=decide_contract_years, gm.year) [OFFSEASON convention, NOT year+1 — see gui_main.cpp:5054], set contract.amount_k=top_bid_k and contract.exp_year = gm.year + yrs - 1 (canonical formula, matches Team.cpp:442/2347), push_poach_news, erase fa_suitors_[p]. If the user's modal still references p, target.reset() is handled because team_name flips off "Free Agent" — add a guard in the modal: if target && target->team_name != "Free Agent" then show "SIGNED ELSEWHERE" banner and force-close.
NEVER-FREE: all keyed by Player*; sign_player only moves the PlayerPtr into a roster vector, never deletes (GameManager.cpp:2158 invariant). Budget always debited explicitly (the FA is free of money only until a bid clears min_acceptable_k).

featureSpecs.formula_or_logic:
PER-DAY LOOP. OFFSEASON runs total_rounds=14 day-rounds (current_phase_pacing, GameManager.cpp:55,892). On each offseason day, advance_day already calls run_offseason_fa_decay() (line 904). We add run_offseason_live_bids() right after it, so decay shaves FA asks first, then rivals bid against the (now-decayed) price — same ordering rationale as the decay/soloQ comment at 899-906.

WHICH FAs ARE COURTED. We do not need a UI handshake: define a courted FA as any region-ladder Free Agent with ovr()>=70 that has a stamped fa_demand_baseline_k_ entry (i.e. it has been sitting in the pool being decayed). This auto-covers exactly the FAs the user can see/court in DrawMarket. (ovr>=70 floor, looser than B8's 75, per the note that a live system should cover more than walked stars.)

PER-FA SUITOR STATE. New map keyed by Player* (mirrors fa_demand_baseline_k_, honoring never-free: Player* persists in the soloQ ladder for the world's life):
  struct LiveSuitor { TeamPtr team; int bid_k=0; int years=0; int deadline_day=0; };
  std::unordered_map<Player*, LiveSuitor> fa_live_suitor_;
deadline_day is an absolute day_in_phase value (0..14).

DAILY BID LOGIC per courted FA p (reusing B8 math at GameManager.cpp:2044-2065):
1. If no suitor yet (not in fa_live_suitor_): with probability gated by demand, try to OPEN one. Reuse B8 candidate collection (GameManager.cpp:2007-2027) but over the FA's region league: skip user_team; require other->budget >= est*1.1*1000 where est = some_team->market_value_estimate(p) (use the FA's own region's first team or any rostered team as the estimator ctx — or simpler, est = p.contract.amount_k since it is the live decayed ask); require positional need other->target_comp.need[role] > have (same loop as 2016-2020); rank by composite = team_import_appetite(*other,role) + budget/4e6 + prestige/200 (2022-2026). Take the top candidate. Its opening bid = gen_contract(year, randomize=true, randomize=false, cand.get()).amount_k, with years = cand->decide_contract_years(p, year). Reject-to-open if bid < propose_resign_offer(*cand, year).min_acceptable_k or cand->budget < bid*1000 (B8 gates 2060-2062). On success store LiveSuitor{cand, bid_k, yrs, deadline_day = day_in_phase + 3} — a 3-day countdown.
2. If a suitor exists and deadline NOT reached: each day RAISE the bid by +8% (escalation), capped at kSalaryCapK=180: bid_k = min(180, round(bid_k*1.08)); re-store. This is the visible pressure ramp.
3. If a suitor exists and day_in_phase >= deadline_day: the deadline FIRES. Run the final gate p.accepts_resign_offer(bid_k, years, *suitor.team) (Player.cpp:3063, the same hard floor gate every signing routes through). If accepted -> RIVAL SIGNS (see implementation). If not accepted, push the deadline out by +3 days and bump bid +8% once more (gives the user/AI another window) OR drop the suitor if bid already at cap.

NUMBERS: escalation +8%/day, countdown 3 days, ovr>=70 courting floor, budget floor est*1.1, cap kSalaryCapK=180, floor gate = accepts_resign_offer (min_acceptable_k = round(ask*0.85), or *0.95 Greedy/Mercenary, Player.cpp:2831). These mirror existing constants; only +8% and the 3-day window are new tunables (declare as static constexpr like kFADecayRate at GameManager.h:529).

NEVER-FREE: no player/team is ever destroyed; the rival sign uses Team::sign_player which only mutates rosters (PlayerPtr stays alive in the soloQ ladder). fa_live_suitor_ holds TeamPtr (shared_ptr) + Player* key, both lifetime-stable. Cleared on reset_world.

After line 534 add decl: `void run_offseason_live_bids(std::vector<std::string>& log);` (takes the same log& advance_day already has in scope).

GameManager.cpp reset_world: after `fa_demand_baseline_k_.clear();` (line 285) add `fa_live_suitor_.clear();` so suitor state never leaks across worlds (matches the header comment at 521-523 about wiping per-offseason state).

GameManager.cpp advance_day OFFSEASON branch: after `run_offseason_fa_decay();` (line 904) and BEFORE the soloQ tick loop at 906, insert `run_offseason_live_bids(log);`. `log` is the std::vector<std::string>& parameter already in advance_day scope (it is passed to run_end_of_year at 875). Place it after decay so bids reference decayed asks; place it before soloQ so a soloQ event can't sign a player mid-bid with a stale price (same reasoning as the existing 899-906 comment).

GameManager.cpp: define `void GameManager::run_offseason_live_bids(std::vector<std::string>& log)` immediately after run_offseason_fa_decay (after line 2444). Loop `for (auto& kv : solo_qs)` then `for (auto& p : kv.second->global_ladder())` exactly like decay (2409-2414): skip null, is_retired, and team_name != "Free Agent". Additional skip: `if (p->ovr() < kLiveBidCourtOvr) continue;` and `if (!fa_demand_baseline_k_.count(p.get())) continue;` (only court FAs already in the decay/visible set).

Inside the loop, branch on `auto sit = fa_live_suitor_.find(p.get());`. NO existing suitor: collect candidates by COPYING B8's filter block (GameManager.cpp:2005-2034) — find the FA's region league via `leagues.find(p->region)`, iterate teams(), skip user_team, require budget headroom (floor_budget = (long long)(p->contract.amount_k * 1.1) * 1000LL — p->contract.amount_k IS the live decayed ask), require target_comp.need[role] > have, rank by composite (2022-2026), sort desc, take front(). Opening bid via `Contract bc = p->gen_contract(year, true, false, cand.get());` and `int yrs = cand->decide_contract_years(*p, year);` and `Player::ResignOffer fresh = p->propose_resign_offer(*cand, year);`. Reject-to-open if `bc.amount_k < fresh.min_acceptable_k` or `cand->budget < (long long)bc.amount_k*1000LL` (B8 gates 2060-2062). Else store `fa_live_suitor_[p.get()] = {cand, bc.amount_k, yrs, day_in_phase + kLiveBidCountdownDays};` and push a news/log line ('[FA INTEREST] <team> opens N-day pursuit of <player> at $XK').

EXISTING suitor, deadline not reached (`day_in_phase < it->second.deadline_day`): escalate `it->second.bid_k = std::min((int)kSalaryCapK, (int)std::round(it->second.bid_k * (1.0 + kLiveBidEscalation)));`. Optional log every raise. This is the per-day pressure the user sees.

EXISTING suitor, deadline reached (`day_in_phase >= it->second.deadline_day`): final gate `if (p->accepts_resign_offer(it->second.bid_k, it->second.years, *it->second.team))` then RIVAL SIGNS — mirror the B8 poach sign block (GameManager.cpp:2084-2097) BUT use gm.year not year+1 because we are INSIDE the OFFSEASON phase (year already incremented; the gui_main.cpp:5054 convention): no release needed (player is already Free Agent), debit budget `it->second.team->budget -= (long long)it->second.bid_k * 1000LL;`, then `it->second.team->sign_player(p, it->second.years, year);` (3-arg overload, Team.h:277), then bake the agreed numbers exactly like B8: `p->contract.amount_k = it->second.bid_k; p->contract.exp_year = year + it->second.years - 1; p->contract_years = it->second.years;` — this preserves the CANONICAL exp_year = first_full_season + years - 1 (here first_full_season = year). Push poach/sign news, then `fa_demand_baseline_k_.erase(p.get());` and `fa_live_suitor_.erase(it);`. If NOT accepted: push deadline `it->second.deadline_day += kLiveBidCountdownDays;` and bump bid once `it->second.bid_k = std::min((int)kSalaryCapK, (int)std::round(it->second.bid_k*(1.0+kLiveBidEscalation)));`; if already at cap (180) and still rejected, `fa_live_suitor_.erase(it);` (suitor gives up).

BUDGET DEBIT is manual (sign_player does NOT debit — GameManager.cpp:2085-2088 comment). The rival sign block above debits explicitly; do not omit or budget leaks. Use the SAME 3-arg sign_player(p, years, year) the user FA path effectively uses; the Role-aware 4-arg overload (Team.h:284) is unnecessary since FAs sign at primary_role (pass nothing => defaults to primary_role per the comment at Team.h:281-283).

USER-FACING DEADLINE: the user reacts by signing the FA themselves through the existing DrawNegotiationModal FA path (gui_main.cpp:7766 -> commit 5025-5070 -> team->sign_player(p,yrs,gm.year)). When the user signs, p->team_name becomes the user team, so on the next offseason day run_offseason_live_bids skips it (team_name != 'Free Agent' guard) and the suitor is naturally abandoned. Optionally erase fa_live_suitor_[p] in the user commit path at gui_main.cpp:5056 for immediate cleanup, but the team_name guard already makes it correct. The rival sign only fires if the user does NOT sign before deadline_day — that is the forcing function.

UI (optional, non-blocking): in DrawNegotiationModal first-frame cache 4796-4802, look up gm.fa_live_suitor_ for target.get() and stash bid_k/deadline_day; render in the LEFT column near 4888 as 'Other interest: <team> bidding $<bid_k>K — <deadline_day - gm.day_in_phase> days left'. Requires a public const accessor on GameManager since fa_live_suitor_ is private; add `const std::unordered_map<Player*,LiveSuitor>& live_suitors() const { return fa_live_suitor_; }` in the public section.

featureSpecs.formula_or_logic:
POD (Player.h, all default-member-initialized so Contract stays an aggregate and `Contract c;`/`contract{}` keep their defaults):

  struct Contract {
      int amount_k = 25;          // unchanged (canonical, keep first)
      int exp_year = 0;           // unchanged (canonical, keep first)
      int signing_bonus_k = 0;    // NEW one-time up-front $K, paid at signing, NOT recurring payroll
      struct Promise {
          bool guaranteed_role = false; // contract_role is locked; switching role later = breach
          bool starter         = false; // promised a starting-5 slot (roster[0..4])
      } promise{};                // NEW nested aggregate member, brace-init keeps Contract aggregate (C++17)
      bool promise_active   = true;   // cleared once the promise is honored/expired so it fires at most per-season
  };

BONUS valuation (evaluate_resign_offer, Player.cpp after salary_mod ~2851). Amortize the up-front bonus over the deal so it reads as effective $/yr, then run it through the SAME monotonic salary scale so the +55 cap and monotonicity invariant (Player.h:621-627) hold:
  eff_per_yr = offer_bonus_k / max(1, years)
  eff_amount = amount_k + eff_per_yr
  recompute ratio = eff_amount / ask  -> bonus contributes via the existing salary_mod curve.
  Implement as: bonus_mod = salary_mod(eff_amount) - salary_mod(amount_k)  (delta, so the existing salary_mod label stays "pure salary" and bonus_mod is its own labeled line). bonus_mod is >=0 always (bonus never lowers score) — preserves monotonicity.
  Numbers: ask=50, amount=50, years=2, bonus=40 -> eff_per_yr=20, eff_amount=70, ratio=1.40 -> over=min(0.60,0.40)=0.40 -> salary-scale +37; pure-salary part 0 -> bonus_mod=+37. Same bonus over 4 years -> eff_per_yr=10 -> +18. Short deals make a bonus worth more (real risk/reward on the years slider).

PROMISE valuation (promise_mod, Player.cpp ~2960, folded as its own label, NOT into desire_mod to keep UI sum correct):
  starter promise:        +12 if role_fit(offered_role) decent (rf>=0.55) else +6 (a benched-bound player values a starter guarantee less because it is less credible)
  guaranteed_role:        +8
  Personality interaction: Greedy/Mercenary value promises LESS (*0.5, they want cash); Competitor/RingChaser value starter promise MORE (+4) because playing time = winning/exposure. Loyal/StabilitySeeker +4 to guaranteed_role (security).
  Cap promise_mod at +20 so it can rescue a marginal deal but not invert salary monotonicity.

YEARS RISK/REWARD becomes real because (a) the amortized bonus rewards SHORT deals (above) while (b) the existing years_mod (dy=|years-ideal|: 0:+6,1:+1,2:-6,>=3:-12, Player.cpp:2925-2934) already punishes long deals vs preference. Net: a big bonus on a short deal scores high; the same bonus stretched long dilutes to near-zero — the slider now trades up-front cost vs commitment.

b.total update (Player.cpp:3011): b.total = base + salary_mod + bonus_mod + prestige_mod + contender_mod + loyalty_mod + years_mod + promise_mod + desire_mod; clamp [0,100]. Hard-block branch (Player.cpp:3016-3028) unchanged — bonus/promise are additive sweeteners, they do NOT clear the min_acceptable_k floor (you still must meet the salary floor; a bonus cannot buy past an insulting base salary, which keeps min_acceptable_k meaningful).

DELAYED BENCHED-PROMISE UNHAPPINESS (save_history_and_progress, Player.cpp:1240, run before season reset; team_name in scope, season_matches is the deterministic benched signal):
  if (contract.promise.starter && contract.promise_active && years_left(year) >= 1 && season_matches == 0) {
      bump_mood(team_name, +0.35);      // mood is "anger at team" in [0,1]; +0.35 = strong grievance
      contract.promise_active = false;  // fire once per season, re-arms only on a new starter season
  }
  Consequence is automatic via existing mood plumbing: mood raises next ask amount_with_mood = base*(1+0.20*mood) (Player.cpp:477) and the dignity floor refuses_to_negotiate floor_k=30+70*mood (Player.cpp:482), and inside evaluate desire_mod -= 22 when refuses fires (Player.cpp:2970-2977). So a benched promise -> next-year re-sign is materially harder/more expensive WITHOUT any new state machine. season_matches>0 (he started) leaves promise_active true and mood untouched — promise honored.

PAYROLL/NEVER-FREE: signing_bonus_k is one-time. Do NOT add it to Team.cpp:717 payroll sum or salary_log (which is (year,amount_k) pairs, positionally fixed at Team.cpp:724 — leave it). Debit it once at the signing call site (gui_main commit + GameManager AI sign paths) exactly like the existing manual budget debit pattern (GameManager.cpp:2088 comment). No allocation/free anywhere — pure POD value writes on already-live shared_ptr<Player>.

featureSpecs.formula_or_logic:
GOAL: fold OVR + recent performance + accolades into ONE coherent value_index that drives both the band selection (value_p) and a single consolidated quality multiplier, replacing the 9-way multiplier stack so accolades/form aren't double-counted (today they enter twice: star_mult+trophy_mult for accolades, avg_match_rating+last_season for form — see NOTES in map).

THREE NEW HELPERS (all pure, const, no rng):

(1) perf_score() in [0.85, 1.30] — RECENT performance, weighted toward this/last season:
  double r_recent = history.empty() ? avg_match_rating() : history.back().rating; // last-season rating ~0.7..1.4
  double r_career = avg_match_rating();                                            // career rating ~0.8..1.3
  double rating = 0.65*r_recent + 0.35*r_career;       // recency-weighted, NOT double-counted
  double kast_pct = (career_rounds>0 ? career_kast_pct() : 70.0); // 0..100, ~60..80 typical
  double clutch = std::min(1.0, (season_clutch_pts + career_grand_final_clutches*2) / 12.0); // 0..1 saturating
  // center each signal at its neutral point, scale, sum, clamp:
  double s = 1.0
           + clamp_v((rating - 1.00) * 0.50, -0.15, 0.22)   // rating: 1.30->+0.15..0.22, 0.85->-0.075, <=0.70 floors -0.15
           + clamp_v((kast_pct - 70.0) / 100.0, -0.05, 0.06) // KAST 80%->+0.06, 60%->-0.05
           + clutch * 0.06;                                  // up to +0.06 for clutch merchants
  return clamp_v(s, 0.85, 1.30);
  RANGE CHECK: breakout star (rating1.30, KAST80, clutch full) -> 1+0.22+0.06+0.06 = 1.30 cap. Slumping bench (rating0.80,KAST62,clutch0) -> 1-0.10-0.04 = 0.86. Neutral (1.00,70,0) -> 1.00.

(2) accolade_score() in [1.00, 1.55] — DURABLE + recent accolades via trophy_summary() (one pass, deduped — see map NOTE preferring it over manual scans):
  TrophySummary ts = trophy_summary(); // {regional,masters,worlds,role_awards,mvps,igl_oty}
  // weighted career trophy points (worlds >> masters >> regional), MVPs separate from award strings to avoid the gen_contract double-count:
  double career_pts = ts.worlds*10.0 + ts.masters*5.0 + ts.regional*2.0
                    + ts.mvps*4.0 + ts.role_awards*2.5 + ts.igl_oty*3.0;
  double durable = std::min(0.40, career_pts / 100.0);     // up to +0.40 for a stacked HoF resume
  // recency bump: reuse the EXISTING current/prior-year award scan already in gen_contract (lines 370-382) value `recent_trophies`,
  // but expose it here instead: scan awards for current_year/current_year-1 substrings, World+4/Masters+2/Regional+1:
  double recent = std::min(0.15, recent_trophies_count() * 0.025); // up to +0.15 for fresh silverware
  return 1.0 + durable + recent;  // [1.00, 1.55]
  (recent_trophies_count() is the existing inline scan factored into a tiny helper; or inline it as today.)
  RANGE CHECK: no trophies -> 1.00. One regional this year -> 1.0+0.02+0.025=1.045. Multi-Worlds MVP vet -> 1.40 (durable cap)+0.15 = 1.55 cap.

(3) value_index() = the band driver, OVR-anchored:
  double value_p = 0.55*ovr() + 0.45*potential;  // UNCHANGED from line 321 — keeps band thresholds (85/70/55/40) valid
  return value_p;
  (Keep value_p exactly as-is so the piecewise base schedule and per-band absolute_max at 395-415 are untouched. The band caps 25/45/75/110/180 remain the true ceiling — see map.)

REPLACE the multiplier stack (Player.cpp:417-426). Keep the AGE/career-phase, leadership, upside, risk, role, veteran multipliers (those are NOT OVR/perf/accolade axes), but REPLACE star_mult(line 419), last_season_mult(line 423), and trophy_mult(line 424) with the two consolidated helpers:

  amount *= phase_mult;                 // keep (AGE U-curve, lines 324-330)
  amount *= perf_score();               // REPLACES last_season_mult (was line 423) AND the rating part of star_mult
  amount *= accolade_score();           // REPLACES trophy_mult (line 424) AND the awards/mvp part of star_mult

Then DELETE the now-dead star_pts/star_mult block (333-339), last_season_mult block (359-366), and trophy_mult block (368-382) — their logic moved into the two helpers. Leave value_p (321), phase (324-330), lead (342-344), upside (347-350), risk (353-355), veteran (386-388) in place.
