featureSpecs.formula_or_logic:
All four scalars are 1..99, neutral at 50. Define a shared helper on Player.h (inline, noexcept, pure) so ask and decision read the SAME coefficient:
  double trait01(int t) const noexcept { return (t - 50) / 49.0; }  // -1.0..+1.0, 0 at neutral

AMBITION (wants winners/role): does NOT touch raw salary. It biases WILLINGNESS toward strong/contender teams and adds a decision penalty toward weak ones.
  - willingness swing (Player.cpp:2674-2749, add after the desire switch): w += 0.20*trait01(ambition) when team_is_contender(team)||avg_roster_ovr(team)>=75; w -= 0.20*trait01(ambition) when avg_roster_ovr(team)<65. So a 99-ambition player gets +0.20/-0.20, a 1-ambition player inverts mildly.
  - desire_mod (Player.cpp:2943-2977 block): if avg_roster_ovr(team) < 65.0: b.desire_mod += round(-18 * max(0.0, trait01(ambition))); (up to -18 at ambition 99, 0 at <=50). Role-ambition reuses existing role_mod (Player.cpp:2988-3004) — no new field needed; an ambitious player offered a bench/off-role already eats role_mod, so scale it: role_mod = round(role_mod * (1.0 + 0.30*max(0.0,trait01(ambition)))) (up to +30% harsher at ambition 99).

LOYALTY (discount to re-sign, resists suitors): only meaningful via same_team. ASK discount when re-signing; willingness boost to keep; suitor resistance.
  - propose ask (Player.cpp:2642-2658): if same_team: amount *= (1.0 - 0.15*max(0.0,trait01(loyalty))); (up to 15% home discount at loyalty 99, stacks with existing Loyal*0.85). Mirror in gen_contract is NOT needed since evaluate uses team_ctx but not same_team discount — keep evaluate's deterministic ask via the SAME multiply at Player.cpp:2822 region so floors agree.
  - loyalty_mod (Player.cpp:2908-2921): if same_team: b.loyalty_mod += round(12 * max(0.0, trait01(loyalty))); (up to +12 to STAY at loyalty 99). If !same_team (rival suitor): b.loyalty_mod -= round(10 * max(0.0, trait01(loyalty))); (up to -10 resisting a poach).
  - suitor switch (GameManager.cpp:2063): rival composite already = willingness*amount; willingness is lowered cross-team for high loyalty via the propose willingness swing: w -= 0.15*max(0.0,trait01(loyalty)) when !same_team.

GREED (higher demands, harsher reject below ask): raises ask and the min-acceptable floor.
  - ask (gen_contract Player.cpp:440 region & propose Player.cpp:2657): amount *= (1.0 + 0.12*max(0.0,trait01(greed))); (up to +12% at greed 99). Apply in gen_contract's team_ctx block so BOTH propose AND evaluate's deterministic ask move together (keeps the two ask paths consistent — the documented drift risk).
  - floor_frac (propose Player.cpp:2753 AND evaluate Player.cpp:2836): floor_frac = base + 0.10*max(0.0,trait01(greed)) where base is the existing 0.85 (or 0.95 stiff). So greed 99 raises a non-stiff floor from 0.85 to 0.95 (less willing to discount). MUST edit BOTH sites identically or the UI breakdown floor and the hard-block diverge.

EGO (harsher reject): pure decision-side reject amplifier on under-ask offers; preserves salary monotonicity (only makes BELOW-ask worse, never makes a higher offer reject).
  - In the desire_mod block (Player.cpp:2943-2977): if amount_k < ask: b.desire_mod -= round(12 * max(0.0, trait01(ego)) * min(1.0, (ask - amount_k)/(double)ask)); (up to -12 at ego 99 and a deep lowball, scaling with how far below ask). Because it ONLY fires when amount_k<ask and shrinks to 0 as amount_k->ask, it cannot make a higher offer score lower — the Player.h:621-627 monotonicity invariant holds.

NEUTRALITY / CONTEXT-FREE PATH: every coefficient uses max(0.0, trait01(x)) for the demand-raising/reject-harshening direction so a 50 (neutral) player is a NO-OP and existing smoke tests #12/#13 salary bands stay in range. gen_contract called with team_ctx==nullptr (ctor seed Player.cpp:132, 3-arg overload) skips ALL of this (the multiplies live inside the existing `if (team_ctx != nullptr)` block), so context-free asks carry neutral personality exactly as today.
implementation: Player.h:320 — after `Desire desire = Desire::Competitor;` add `int ambition = 50; int loyalty = 50; int greed = 50; int ego = 50;` with a comment matching the Desire doc-block (rolled once at spawn, persist for career, 1..99 neutral 50).
Player.h (near the other inline accessors, e.g. by years_left ~Player.h:395) — add `inline double trait01(int t) const noexcept { return (t - 50) / 49.0; }`.

Player.cpp generate_player (the function that calls pick_spawn_desire at Player.cpp:2423 and assigns p.desire) — after the desire assignment, set the four scalars from attrs/age/desire. Suggested seeds (all clamp to [1,99]): greed = clamp(40 + at(attrs,Attr::Aggressiveness)/3 + (desire==Greedy?25:desire==Mercenary?15:0) + rng noise); ego = clamp(35 + at(attrs,Attr::Aggressiveness)/3 - consistency/4 + (desire==Mercenary||desire==Greedy?20:0)); loyalty = clamp(45 + (at(attrs,Attr::Communication)+at(attrs,Attr::Adaptability))/8 + (desire==Loyal?30:desire==Mercenary?-25:0)); ambition = clamp(45 + at(attrs,Attr::Clutch)/5 + (age<25?10:0) + (desire==RingChaser||desire==Competitor?20:0)). This is the SINGLE ask site that both propose and evaluate route through, so greed moves both consistently.

Keep the final clamp_v(w,0.05,0.95) at Player.cpp:2749.

`amount_k` (the player's current SALARY in $K) lives ON the Contract, accessed as `p->contract.amount_k` everywhere (Team.cpp:717 payroll sum, :724 salary_log append, :809/:998/:1000/:2250 valuation math, :1735/:1812/:2346 writes at signing, smoke_test.cpp:220-221 floor/cap asserts 10..180).
`exp_year` (the LIVE 'years remaining' counter source) lives ON the Contract, accessed as `p->contract.exp_year`. Written ONLY at signing/resign: Team.cpp:442, :2347, and Player.cpp:465.

CANONICAL exp_year formula = first_full_season + years - 1. Realised three times identically: Player.cpp:465 `c.exp_year = base_year + years - 1;` (base_year = current_year>0 ? current_year : current_world_year()), Team.cpp:442 `p->contract.exp_year = sign_year + years - 1;`, Team.cpp:2347 `p->contract.exp_year = current_year + years - 1;`. smoke_test.cpp:1281 asserts `contract.exp_year == yr + yl - 1`.

`contract_years` is a SEPARATE Player member (Player.h:372 `int contract_years = 1;`), NOT on the Contract. It is a frozen SNAPSHOT of the signed duration, set once per signing (Team.cpp:2348 `p->contract_years = years;`) and NEVER decremented. Documented at Player.h:364-372 and Team.cpp:1480 as the signed-duration snapshot, distinct from the live exp_year counter.
`contract_exp_year` as a standalone field does NOT exist; the canonical expiry lives at `contract.exp_year`. The query name maps to Contract::exp_year.

`years_left(int current_year)` is an inline noexcept method on Player (Player.h:395-398): returns 0 if team_name=='Free Agent' or 'Retired', else `std::max(0, contract.exp_year - current_year + 1)`. This is the canonical 'years remaining' accessor; callers must use it, NOT contract_years.

`salary_log` is a Player member at Player.h:401 `std::vector<std::pair<int,int>> salary_log;` storing (year, amount_k) pairs; appended in Team.cpp:724 `p->salary_log.emplace_back(year, p->contract.amount_k);`.

`requested_salary_k` does NOT exist on Player or Contract. The only `requested_salary_k()` in the codebase is a Coach method (Coach.h:72, Coach.cpp:24) — unrelated. The Player-side 'asking salary' concept is instead Player::ResignOffer::amount_k (Player.h:591) plus gen_contract() output.

`market_value` as a named field does NOT exist. The market-value concept is computed on demand: Team.cpp:2366-2374 `estimate_market_value` calls `p.gen_contract(0, randomize_amount=true, randomize_exp=false, this)` and returns `c.amount_k`. Also Player::get_transfer_value() (Player.h:528) is the transfer-fee estimator.
Player ctor seeds the contract at Player.cpp:132-133: `Contract c0 = gen_contract(0, true, true); contract = c0;` (current_year=0 so base_year falls back to current_world_year()).

notes_for_rebuild: AGGREGATE-INIT SAFETY: Contract is a true aggregate (no ctors, all public, default member initializers). CRUCIALLY, no call site uses braced aggregate-init `Contract{a, b}` — grep for `Contract{`/`Contract {` found ZERO positional-brace constructions. Every construction is `Contract c{};` or `Contract c;` followed by field assignment (Player.cpp:458, Team.cpp:2370), or value-init `contract{}` (Player.h:388). Therefore adding NEW fields with default member initializers is fully backward-safe: existing `Contract c;` and `contract{}` keep compiling, and the new fields silently take their defaults.

RULE: give every new field a default member initializer (e.g. `int signing_bonus_k = 0;`). This preserves both aggregate-ness AND value-init behavior; no existing call site breaks because none relies on positional ordering.

To add a Promise sub-struct: declare a nested `struct Promise { ... all members default-initialized ...; };` ABOVE its use inside Contract (or as a sibling top-level struct in the vlr namespace before Contract at Player.h:246), then add `Promise promise{};` as a Contract member. A nested aggregate member with a brace-or-equal initializer keeps Contract an aggregate (C++17). Do NOT give Promise a user-declared constructor if you want to retain aggregate-init for Promise itself; default member initializers are fine.

Place new fields AFTER the existing two (amount_k, exp_year) for minimal churn — though ordering is irrelevant to existing call sites since none use positional aggregate-init. Keep amount_k and exp_year first to keep the canonical formula sites (Player.cpp:465, Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347) untouched.

CANONICAL INVARIANT to preserve: exp_year = first_full_season + years - 1, duplicated at 3 write sites (Player.cpp:465, Team.cpp:442, Team.cpp:2347) and asserted at smoke_test.cpp:1281. If signing_bonus_k or Promise need to be set at signing, set them at these SAME three sites (Team::sign_player ~Team.cpp:442, Team::resign_player ~Team.cpp:2347, and the gen_contract default/seed at Player.cpp:458-466).

DISTINCTION trap: `contract_years` (Player.h:372) is the frozen snapshot; `contract.exp_year` (Player.h:248) is the live counter. years_left() (Player.h:395) derives remaining years from exp_year, NOT contract_years. Any new Promise/bonus logic that wants 'years remaining' MUST call years_left(year), not read contract_years.

There is NO existing requested_salary_k or market_value field — if the rebuild needs them, they must be ADDED. Today 'asking salary' = gen_contract(...).amount_k / ResignOffer::amount_k, and 'market value' = Team::estimate_market_value (Team.cpp:2366) wrapping gen_contract, plus Player::get_transfer_value (Player.h:528). gen_contract returns Contract BY VALUE via named-object + field assignment (Player.cpp:458-466). When extending Contract, update this builder to initialize any new field you want non-default (otherwise the returned Contract carries the field defaults — usually fine).

A rebuild moving to a continuous curve must preserve the rough output ranges (elite ~80-180K, mid ~30-60K, bench 10-18K) per the 2026-05 rebalance (PROJECT_GUIDE §4.4/§7).

b.total is the clamped sum of base + all mods (Player.cpp:3011-3014): `b.total = b.base_score + b.salary_mod + b.prestige_mod + b.contender_mod + b.loyalty_mod + b.years_mod + b.desire_mod;` then clamped to [0,100]. base_score defaults to 50 (Player.h:629).

Two HARD blocks override the score (Player.cpp:3016-3028): if amount_k < ro.min_acceptable_k OR years < 1 OR years > ro.max_acceptable_years, then `b.total = std::min(b.total, kResignAcceptThreshold - 1)` (forces total to <=54 → guaranteed reject) and sets a reject_reason. This is where a REJECT-COST / counter-offer should likely fire FIRST since it pins the deal below threshold deterministically.

min_acceptable_k = round(ask * 0.85), or *0.95 for Greedy/Mercenary (`stiff`), clamped to [kSalaryFloorK, kSalaryCapK] (Player.cpp:2831-2837). ask = det.amount_k from a DETERMINISTIC gen_contract(randomize=false) call (Player.cpp:2817-2822) — must stay deterministic because the UI calls this every frame.
max_acceptable_years = min(5, year_bucket+1) where year_bucket comes from desire_length_pref(team) lp bucketed 1..4 at lp<=0.25/0.50/0.75 (Player.cpp:2825-2830).

Willingness feeds in via three channels, NOT a single 'willingness' scalar: (1) desire archetype gates that subtract from desire_mod (Player.cpp:2943-2969); (2) refuses_to_negotiate(amount_k, team.name) mood floor → desire_mod -= 22 (Player.cpp:2973-2977); (3) desire_length_pref(team) shaping both the years bands and min_acceptable_k stiffness.

notes_for_rebuild: DRIFT RISK (real): propose_resign_offer and evaluate_resign_offer each independently call gen_contract and re-derive min_acceptable_k instead of evaluate reading propose's ResignOffer. They diverge by construction: propose uses randomize_amount=TRUE and current_year=actual; evaluate uses randomize_amount=FALSE and current_year=0. The randomized vs deterministic ask differ by up to ±10-20% market noise (gen_contract lines 428-432), so propose.min_acceptable_k and evaluate.ro.min_acceptable_k can disagree by that margin on any given call — the UI breakdown's floor is NOT the same number the displayed ask implies. The comment at Player.cpp:2832-2834 claims they match 'so the UI breakdown and the engine decision never disagree', but they only match in FORMULA (0.85/0.95 * round * clamp), not in the underlying ask value. A rebuild should compute the ask ONCE (propose_resign_offer) and have evaluate take the ResignOffer (or amount_k floor) as a parameter — single source of truth.

Also note current_year mismatch: propose's tenure/trophy windows use the passed current_year; evaluate's tenure loop uses current_world_year() (Player.cpp:2917) and its trophy check has no year context at all (admitted in comment 2882-2887, falls back to 'any trophy present'). So loyalty/contender scoring in evaluate can over-credit stale awards vs propose.

DOUBLE-COUNTING of desire on salary: desire_salary_mult is applied inside gen_contract (line 440) AND propose adds its own Loyal/RingChaser/Mercenary multipliers (2642-2655). Mercenary effectively gets ~1.18*1.10; Loyal+same_team ~1.05*0.85. If reworking, consolidate into one desire-salary path.

WHERE personality/ambition SHOULD bias the demand: today only Desire (archetype) and team-side fields (team.personality, team.strategy, team.prestige, window) bias the ask. There is no player-level 'ambition' trait feeding the demand. Inject it at: (1) gen_contract's multiplier chain (Player.cpp:417-426) for the base ask — an ambitious/high-ego player asks above market; (2) propose_resign_offer.amount adjustment block (Player.cpp:2642-2658) alongside the Desire multipliers; (3) the floor_frac / min_acceptable_k computation (Player.cpp:2753) — a high-ambition player should have a HIGHER floor_frac (less willing to discount); (4) willingness swings (Player.cpp:2674-2749) — ambition lowers willingness toward non-contenders. Any change to the demand/floor MUST be mirrored in evaluate_resign_offer (2831-2837 floor, 2936-2977 desire gates) or the two will diverge further.

willingness is largely vestigial in the keep/walk decision — only the AI rival-bid comparison (GameManager.cpp:2063) and three UI progress bars read it. The actual accept/reject is the additive score in evaluate_resign_offer vs the fixed 55 threshold. If ambition/personality should change whether a player STAYS, it must move the evaluate score (desire_mod / loyalty_mod), not just willingness.
Year bucketing is duplicated: propose (2665-2669) and evaluate (2826-2830) both bucket desire_length_pref independently with the same boundaries — keep them in sync or factor into one helper.

kResignAcceptThreshold (55) is file-local static in Player.cpp:2799 — not visible to GameManager; the AI relies on accepts_resign_offer, which is correct, but any tuning of the threshold only lives in Player.cpp.

maps.component: Player personality/temperament fields + mood + refuses_to_negotiate / dignity-floor (Valosim cpp)
current_behavior: NO scalar personality stats exist. There is NO `ambition`, `loyalty`, `greed`, `work_ethic`-as-personality, `ego`, `professionalism`, `morale`, or `temperament` numeric field anywhere on Player. Grep over Player.h for those terms returns only comments about the `Desire` enum. Personality is encoded ONLY as: (a) the categorical `Desire` enum, (b) the categorical `Archetype` enum, and (c) the per-team `mood` double map. (cpp/include/Player.h, confirmed by grep)

`mood` is a per-team double in [0.0,1.0], default empty map. cpp/include/Player.h:400 `FreeAgentMood mood{}` -> struct at :279-281 holds `std::unordered_map<std::string,double> per_team`. mood_for() returns 0.0 when team absent. (Player.cpp:469-472)

mood currently changes ONLY via bump_mood(team_name, delta): `m = clamp_v(m + delta, 0.0, 1.0)` — additive, clamped 0..1 (Player.cpp:473-476). Decayed globally by decay_mood(delta=0.015 default), subtracting per team clamped to 0..1 (Player.cpp:493-495). No other writer.
mood RAISES the player's ask: amount_with_mood = base * (1 + 0.20*mood), clamped [kSalaryFloorK,kSalaryCapK]. So max +20% at mood=1.0 (Player.cpp:477-481).

refuses_to_negotiate(offer_k, team_name): if mood<=0 returns false (never refuses). Else computes a DIGNITY FLOOR `floor_k = 30.0 + 70.0*mood` ($30K at mood~0, $100K at mood=1.0) and returns true (refuses) iff offer_k < floor_k. Higher offer monotonically REDUCES refusal; higher mood (madder) RAISES the floor. (Player.cpp:482-492)

In the negotiation breakdown evaluate_resign_offer, refuses_to_negotiate firing applies a soft `b.desire_mod -= 22` (not a hard block) so an overpay can mend bad blood (Player.cpp:2970-2977). The HARD block is separate: amount_k < min_acceptable_k OR years out of [1,max_acceptable_years] (Player.cpp:3016-3028).

The DESIRE enum is the real demand/decision-weighting system today. desire_salary_mult returns ~0.85..1.20 by archetype+team context (Player.cpp:2533-2566). desire_accepts is a hard accept/reject gate by archetype (Player.cpp:2568-2611). desire_length_pref returns 0..1 length preference (Player.cpp:2613+). Archetype-specific desire_mod hard-gates live in evaluate_resign_offer at Player.cpp:2943-2968 (StabilitySeeker/RingChaser/Competitor/Greedy/Mercenary).
decay_demands() lowers the stored ask over time independently of mood: 33% chance -1, then *0.995, floored at kSalaryFloorK (Player.cpp:496-501).

notes_for_rebuild: To weight demands AND decisions by personality, you must ADD new scalar fields — none of ambition/loyalty/greed/ego/professionalism/morale exist today. The current system is purely categorical (Desire enum) + a single per-team mood scalar. There is no general-purpose 'morale' or 'happiness' scalar to reuse.

Reuse candidates if you don't want new fields: `work_ethic`/`consistency`/`potential` (1..99) are unused by negotiation and could be repurposed/read; the IGL `tend_*` 0..100 sliders exist but are IGL-only and semantically about playstyle, not contracts.

Monotonicity invariant is load-bearing: evaluate_resign_offer guarantees score is non-decreasing in amount_k (Player.h:621-627 doc). refuses_to_negotiate was previously bugged (offer*mood>100 inverted it); the current floor formula `30+70*mood` deliberately makes higher offers reduce refusal. Any new personality weighting on salary_mod must preserve this — only LENGTH or hard archetype/role gates may make a higher offer fail.

refuses_to_negotiate takes a string team_name (NOT Team*) by design, so it cannot reach team strategy/personality. Team-aware logic must go through desire_accepts/desire_salary_mult/desire_length_pref (which take const Team&). Callers are expected to AND refuses_to_negotiate && desire_accepts (Player.h:676-691).

mood is the ONLY mutable, decaying emotional state. It only ever influences salary ask (+0..20%) and the dignity floor. If personality should modulate mood swings, bump_mood is the single choke point (Player.cpp:473) — e.g. scale delta by a new sensitivity/ego field there.

Desire is assigned ONCE at spawn (pick_spawn_desire) and is stable for career; same for archetype/rookie_archetype. New personality scalars should follow the same 'roll once at generate_player, persist' convention.

All the archetype hard-gates (StabilitySeeker/RingChaser/Competitor/Greedy/Mercenary) live inline in evaluate_resign_offer at Player.cpp:2943-2968 and feed b.desire_mod. That's the central extension point for adding personality-weighted decision penalties; role-fit penalty is folded into desire_mod at :2988-3004.
kSalaryFloorK / kSalaryCapK / kResignAcceptThreshold are the relevant constants (referenced in Player.cpp; defined in Common.h/Player.cpp consts) — clamps and the accept threshold for total score.

notes_for_rebuild: ovr() is recomputed from attributes every call (cheap, pure) and clamped to 95 — it is NOT a stored field. For a stable career-quality signal prefer career_max_ovr (peak) or avg_match_rating() (form) over instantaneous ovr().

career_mvps is a separate int counter from the awards vector. Do NOT double-count: gen_contract already adds career_mvps*4 AND awards.size()*5 (which includes MVP award strings). If you add accolade weight, decide whether to read career_mvps or trophy_summary().mvps, not both.

Accolade counts come in two flavors: the dedicated counters (career_mvps, HoF counters) and prefix-parsed award strings ([T]/[M]/[W]/[A]). trophy_summary() is the single best aggregate — one pass, returns all categories deduped. Prefer it over manual awards scanning.

Award strings are year-suffixed ("[W] World Champion YYYY"). gen_contract's recent_trophy_premium keys off the current/prev year substring (Player.cpp:370-382) — a model for time-weighted accolades (recent titles worth more).

Season counters (season_*) reset at year-end inside save_history_and_progress (Player.cpp:1240+); career_* and awards persist. For contract value use career_* / awards / trophy_summary for durable weight and season_*/is_form_at_risk()/last history.back().rating for recent-form weight.

All listed helpers are pure read-only (const, no rng, no mutation) — safe to call from the contract-value path. trophy_summary/best_season/top_n_seasons walk the awards/history vectors so they are O(awards) / O(history), not O(1); cache if called per-frame.
best_season()/top_n_seasons() gate on history entry matches>=8 (MatchHistoryEntry, Player.h:130-149) — sub-8-match seasons are excluded, so they reflect only 'qualified' seasons.

The existing weighting hub is gen_contract (Player.cpp:301-467). Extend star_mult / add a new accolade_mult there rather than introducing a parallel path, so smoke tests #12/#13 salary bands stay in range (multipliers are deliberately modest, ~0.80-1.30).

WHERE LIVE COMPETING SUITORS PLUG IN: Two complementary spots. (1) For a USER-TARGETED FA with a deadline, the natural home is the OFFSEASON per-day loop (GameManager.cpp:888-950) — add a per-day rival-bid tick alongside run_offseason_fa_decay()(904) that, for FAs the user is courting, lets rival teams place escalating bids with a countdown; if the deadline passes a rival signs via the same Team::sign_player path. (2) The signing/comparison LOGIC already exists in B8 (GameManager.cpp:2044-2092): reuse the Candidate collection (region/budget-floor/positional-need filter 2007-2027) and the Bid winner pick composite=willingness*amount_k (2063,2067-2071), plus the accepts_resign_offer final gate (2075). The existing bidding/willingness*amount comparison is at GameManager.cpp:2063.

B8 vs a LIVE feature differ in two ways: B8 fires ONCE at year-end (instantaneous, no user counter-window) and only for WALKED stars ovr>=75. A live-suitor system needs to (a) run incrementally across the 14 offseason days so the user can react, and (b) cover any FA the user is bidding on, not just ovr>=75 walked players. Reuse B8's candidate/bid math but drive it from the OFFSEASON day loop with persistent per-FA deadline state.

BUDGET DEBIT is manual: sign_player does NOT debit budget (GameManager.cpp:2085-2088 comment); every signing call site debits explicitly (poach 2088, user 5052, resign via resign_player). A new suitor sign must debit team->budget itself or budget leaks.

CONTRACT YEAR PITFALL (2026-05-29 fix, repeated at 1951-1958, 2089-2096, 5054, 2196-2202): signings done INSIDE run_end_of_year pass year+1 (contract for upcoming season); signings done DURING OFFSEASON phase pass gm.year (year already incremented). A live offseason-day suitor sign must use gm.year (the OFFSEASON convention, see gui_main.cpp:5054), NOT year+1.

User team is excluded from AI roster mutation everywhere (GameManager.cpp:2010 B8 skips user_team; 2195 ai_full_offseason_pass skips user unless user_auto_manage) — a live-suitor feature competing AGAINST the user is the intended inverse: rivals act on a pool FA the user wants, which is allowed because the FA is not on the user roster.

ai_full_offseason_pass(fa_pool, year, log) (Team.cpp) is the deep per-team GM fill that consumes the regional FA pool at GameManager.cpp:2202 and as the user safety net at 924 — if live suitors deplete the pool before this runs, ordering matters: B8 + any new live-bid logic currently precede the cleanup (2107) and the ai_full_offseason_pass fill (2195). For an offseason-DAY suitor system the fill has already happened at year-end, so per-day suitors would be competing over leftover pool FAs.

willingness is clamped 0.05-0.95 (Player.cpp:2749) and is same_team-sensitive (loyalty +0.30 for re-sign, penalties cross-team); for a rival suitor same_team is false so willingness skews lower — expect rivals to need higher amount_k to win the composite. min_acceptable_k floor (Player.cpp:2751-2755) is the hard reject line every bid must clear.
